meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first production-ready app.
content.answer_block: End project paralysis by shifting from manual boilerplate to a modular SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you automate the infrastructure layer. This allows you to focus strictly on business logic, ensuring your projects reach deployment instead of languishing in local folders.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and abandoned 'Hello World' repositories. You start with high energy, only to hit the wall of authentication, database schema migrations, and payment integration—the invisible friction that kills momentum before you ever reach the core value proposition of your application.

It is time to stop reinventing the wheel. By adopting an industrial-grade SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your focus for the features that actually matter. This guide transforms you from a student stuck in tutorial hell into an AI-augmented builder capable of shipping production-ready code with the speed of a senior engineering team.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Domain
steps.description: Define your SaaS boundaries before writing a single line of code. Use Antigravity to generate a scalable domain model that accounts for multi-tenancy from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Design a multi-tenant SaaS schema for a [Project Name]. Include User, Organization, and Subscription roles. Output strictly in Prisma schema format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a Prisma schema based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Automating Authentication Flow
steps.description: Auth is a bottleneck. Use your starter kit's pre-built auth module, but use Claude Code to wire it into your specific user roles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an Auth flow using NextAuth.js for a SaaS starter kit, ensuring RBAC integration with the previously defined Prisma schema.

steps.title: Implementing Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Don't build billing from scratch. Use the starter kit's webhooks to handle subscription lifecycle events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a Stripe webhook handler that triggers a database update in the UserSubscription table upon 'invoice.payment_succeeded'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate the Stripe webhook handler into the existing API route structure.'

steps.title: Developing Feature Modules
steps.description: Isolate your core features into reusable components. This keeps your codebase maintainable as you iterate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a modular component structure for a dashboard feature that fetches data via server actions and displays it in a responsive grid.

steps.title: CI/CD and Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Your project isn't real until it's live. Automate deployment to Vercel or Railway to ensure continuous shipping.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests before deploying to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup production deployment workflow for Vercel with environment variable injection.'

pro_tips: Always define your database schema before writing frontend components; it is the single source of truth for your SaaS.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API routes immediately after creation to prevent regression during rapid iteration.
Avoid over-engineering the UI; leverage a component library like shadcn/ui to maintain a premium look without custom CSS overhead.
Implement feature flags early so you can ship incomplete features to production without breaking the user experience.
Treat your logs as your primary debugging tool; integrate an observability service like Axiom or Sentry before your first beta tester arrives.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, gather feedback from at least three users, use Claude Code to refactor based on their friction points, and ship your first update within 48 hours.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your brilliant ideas die in a private repository. Join our community to access exclusive starter templates, peer code reviews, and the support you need to push to production. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
